commands.descriptionHtml: Show the Docker client and engine versions
Display system-wide info about the Docker engine, storage driver, and resources

commands.searchDescription: Get help on a Docker command
commands.descriptionHtml: Show help and available flags for any Docker command
Sign in to Docker Hub so you can push and pull private images
Authenticate against a private registry (e.g. ghcr.io, registry.gitlab.com)
Remove stored credentials for Docker Hub from the local config
Show how much disk space images, containers, volumes, and build cache are using
List all configured Docker contexts (local, remote, swarm)
Download an image from a registry (defaults to Docker Hub and the latest tag)
Pull a specific image tag — e.g. node:20-alpine
List all images stored locally with their size and tags
List only dangling (untagged) images that can usually be removed safely
Build an image from the Dockerfile in the current directory

commands.codeHtml: docker build -t <name>:<tag> .
commands.descriptionHtml: Build an image and tag it with a name and version
Build using a Dockerfile at a custom path (useful for monorepos or Dockerfile.dev)

commands.codeHtml: docker build --no-cache -t <name> .
commands.descriptionHtml: Build from scratch ignoring all cached layers — use when dependencies look stale
Pass a build-time variable (matches an ARG in the Dockerfile)

commands.codeHtml: docker build --target <stage> -t <name> .
commands.descriptionHtml: Build only up to a named stage in a multi-stage Dockerfile
Create an additional tag for an existing image (e.g. tag for a registry before push)
Push a tagged image to a registry — must be logged in
⚠️ Remove a local image — fails if any container still uses it
⚠️ Force-remove an image even if containers reference it
Show low-level metadata for an image as JSON (layers, env, entrypoint, labels)
Show the layer-by-layer build history of an image (great for debugging size)
Save one or more images to a tar archive (useful for offline transfer)
Load images from a tar archive previously created with docker save
Search Docker Hub for images matching a term

commands.searchDescription: Run a new container from image
commands.descriptionHtml: Create and start a new container from an image (foreground)
Run a container detached, in the background
Open an interactive TTY shell inside a new container
Automatically remove the container when it exits (great for one-off jobs)
Assign a friendly name to the container instead of the random one

commands.searchDescription: Map host port to container port
commands.codeHtml: docker run -p <host>:<container> <image>
commands.descriptionHtml: Publish a container port to a host port — e.g. -p 8080:80
Publish all EXPOSEd ports to random host ports
Mount a volume or a host directory into the container
Modern, explicit syntax for bind mounts and volume mounts
Set an environment variable inside the container
Load environment variables from a file (one KEY=value per line)
Connect the container to a specific user-defined network
Set a restart policy: no, on-failure, always, or unless-stopped
Set the working directory inside the container

commands.codeHtml: docker run -u <uid>:<gid> <image>
commands.descriptionHtml: Run as a specific user / group instead of the image default (often root)
Cap container resource usage — memory and CPU shares
Override the image's default CMD with a custom command
Create a container but don't start it (use docker start later)
List running containers with their IDs, ports, and status
List all containers — running and stopped
Print only container IDs (great for scripting — docker rm $(docker ps -aq))
Start one or more stopped containers
Gracefully stop a running container (sends SIGTERM, then SIGKILL after grace period)
Stop and then start a container
Pause all processes inside the container (SIGSTOP)
Resume processes inside a paused container
⚠️ Send SIGKILL — terminate a container immediately without graceful shutdown
Remove a stopped container — frees the name and metadata
⚠️ Force-remove a running container (kills then removes)
⚠️ Remove every stopped container in one shot
Rename an existing container
Block until a container stops, then print its exit code
⚠️ Remove all stopped containers — asks for confirmation
Print all stdout / stderr emitted by a container
Follow logs live as they are emitted (like tail -f)

commands.searchDescription: Show last N lines of logs
commands.descriptionHtml: Show only the last N lines of the container's logs
Show only log lines newer than a duration (e.g. 10m, 1h)
Open an interactive shell inside a running container
Run a one-off command inside a running container
Attach your terminal to the main process of a running container (Ctrl+P Ctrl+Q to detach)
Dump full JSON metadata for a container, image, network, or volume
Extract a single field from inspect output using a Go template
Live CPU, memory, network, and IO stats for all running containers
Show the running processes inside a container
List the port mappings for a container
Show files added, changed, or deleted inside a container vs. its image

commands.searchDescription: Copy file from host to container
commands.descriptionHtml: Copy a file or directory from the host into a container

commands.searchDescription: Copy file from container to host
commands.descriptionHtml: Copy a file or directory from a container back to the host
Create a named volume managed by Docker
List all volumes on the host
Show details about a volume — including the mountpoint on the host
⚠️ Remove a named volume — only works if no container is using it
⚠️ Remove all unused (dangling) volumes — data loss if a volume is unintentionally orphaned
Mount a named volume into the container — data persists across restarts

commands.codeHtml: docker run -v $(pwd):/app <image>
commands.descriptionHtml: Bind-mount a host directory into the container (live edits show up inside)
Mount an in-memory tmpfs filesystem inside the container — wiped on stop
Create a user-defined bridge network — containers on it can talk by name

commands.codeHtml: docker network create --driver bridge <name>
commands.descriptionHtml: Create a network with a specific driver (bridge, overlay, macvlan, …)
List all Docker networks
Show details of a network — connected containers, subnet, gateway
Connect a running container to an additional network
Disconnect a container from a network
Remove a user-defined network (must have no attached containers)
⚠️ Remove all unused user-defined networks

commands.searchDescription: Start all services in compose file
commands.descriptionHtml: Build (if needed) and start all services defined in compose.yaml
Start the stack detached, in the background
Force a rebuild of all images before starting services
Start only a named service (and its dependencies)
Stop and remove containers and the default network created by compose
⚠️ Tear down the stack and delete its named volumes — destroys persisted data
Build (or rebuild) all images defined in the compose file
List containers managed by the current compose project
Show logs from all services in the compose project
Follow live logs from a specific service
Run a command inside a running service (e.g. bash, npm test)

commands.searchDescription: Run one off command in new service container
commands.codeHtml: docker compose run --rm <service> <command>
commands.descriptionHtml: Spin up a new one-off container from a service definition (auto-removed)
Restart all services in the project
Stop services without removing the containers
Pull the latest version of every image referenced in the compose file
Validate the compose file and print the fully-rendered, merged configuration
Use a custom or override compose file (repeat -f to merge several)
Enable a named profile so its services are included in the run
Set the base image for the build (must be the first instruction)
Start a named stage in a multi-stage build (great for tiny final images)
Run a command in a new layer at build time (e.g. install packages)
Copy files or directories from the build context into the image
Copy artifacts from an earlier build stage (multi-stage builds)

commands.searchDescription: Add files supports URLs and tar
commands.descriptionHtml: Like COPY but also supports URLs and auto-extracts tarballs (prefer COPY when possible)
Set the working directory for following RUN, CMD, COPY instructions
Set an environment variable available at build and run time
Define a build-time argument (passed via --build-arg)
Document the port the container listens on (does not publish — use -p for that)
Set the default command for the container (overridable on docker run)
Set the executable that always runs — arguments come from CMD or run args
Declare a mount point that should be persisted as a volume
Switch the user for following instructions and the running container (avoid root in production)

commands.codeHtml: HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s CMD curl -f http://localhost/ || exit 1
commands.descriptionHtml: Define how Docker should check that the container is healthy
Add metadata to the image (maintainer, version, source URL, …)
Override the shell used by following RUN, CMD, ENTRYPOINT in shell form
Set the system call signal sent to the container to stop it
Add a trigger that runs when this image is used as a base for another build
Authenticate against Docker Hub with a specific username
Authenticate against a private registry — e.g. GHCR, GitLab, Harbor

commands.codeHtml: docker tag <image> <registry>/<user>/<name>:<tag>
commands.descriptionHtml: Tag a local image with the full path required by a remote registry before pushing
Push a tagged image to a registry — must be logged in first
Pull an image from a private or third-party registry
Remove stored credentials for a specific registry
⚠️ Remove all stopped containers, unused networks, dangling images, and build cache
⚠️ Same as prune but also removes all images not used by any container
⚠️ Nuclear cleanup — also deletes unused volumes (potential data loss)
⚠️ Remove dangling (untagged) images
⚠️ Remove every image not currently used by any container
⚠️ Remove all stopped containers
Remove the BuildKit build cache (frees disk after lots of builds)
Stop every currently running container in one shot
